enrichment.short_description: Cloud platform for automated and manual web and mobile app testing across thousands of real and virtual devices and browsers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sauce Labs is a cloud test platform for web and mobile apps, offering real/virtual device testing, visual testing, and error reporting.

features.title: AI-driven test authoring
features.description: Generates and updates automated tests across web and mobile platforms.

features.title: Real device cloud
features.description: Access to 9,000+ real iOS and Android devices for mobile app testing.

features.title: Virtual device cloud
features.description: 2,500+ emulator, simulator, and browser/OS combinations for cross-browser and cross-device testing.

features.title: Visual testing
features.description: UI regression detection with monthly snapshot allowances by plan.

features.title: Error reporting
features.description: Crash detection and analysis for mobile and web applications.

features.title: Mobile app distribution
features.description: Beta testing and app distribution with configurable app and tester limits.

features.title: Framework support
features.description: Works with Selenium, Appium, Cypress, and Playwright.

pros: Very large real and virtual device/browser inventory (9,000+ real devices, 2,500+ virtual combinations)
Supports major automation frameworks (Selenium, Appium, Cypress, Playwright)
Includes visual testing, error reporting, and app distribution alongside core test execution
Free trial available on all paid plans
Used by large enterprises including Bank of America, Microsoft, and Walmart
cons: Entry-level plans are limited to a single parallel test
Sauce Error Reporting and Sauce Visual are priced separately from core test plans
Enterprise-grade features (SSO, private device cloud, professional services) require custom/Enterprise pricing
Full pricing detail for add-on products is not published and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Does Sauce Labs offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial is available for sign-up across the Live Testing, Virtual Device Cloud, and Real Device Cloud plans.

faqs.question: What automation frameworks does Sauce Labs support?
faqs.answer: Sauce Labs supports Selenium, Appium, Cypress, and Playwright.

faqs.question: How many parallel tests do the base plans include?
faqs.answer: The Live Testing, Virtual Device Cloud, and Real Device Cloud plans each include 1 parallel test by default; higher concurrency requires upgrading.

faqs.question: Does Sauce Labs test on real devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Real Device Cloud plan provides access to thousands of real Android and iOS devices.

faqs.question: Is there an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enterprise pricing is custom and includes SSO, unified analytics, a private device cloud, and premium support.

use_cases.title: Cross-browser web testing
use_cases.description: Run automated and manual tests across thousands of desktop browser and OS combinations.

use_cases.title: Mobile app testing on real devices
use_cases.description: Validate iOS and Android apps on physical devices before release.

use_cases.title: Visual regression testing
use_cases.description: Detect unintended UI changes across releases using automated screenshot comparison.

use_cases.title: Enterprise-scale test operations
use_cases.description: Centralize test execution, analytics, and device access for large engineering organizations.

features: Desktop browsers, mobile emulators and simulators
Thousands of real Android and iOS devices

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sauce Labs?
paragraphs: Sauce Labs is a cloud-based testing platform that lets teams run automated and manual tests for web and mobile applications across thousands of real devices, virtual devices, and browser combinations.
The platform includes visual testing, error reporting, and mobile app distribution, and supports popular automation frameworks including Selenium, Appium, Cypress, and Playwright.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sauce Labs is for
paragraphs: Sauce Labs is built for QA, engineering, and DevOps teams that need to validate web and mobile applications at scale, from individual developers running manual live tests to large enterprises running high-volume automated test suites.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud platform for automated, manual, and visual testing across 6,100+ real browsers and devices, with codeless AI testing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TestingBot offers cloud cross-browser and mobile testing on 6,100+ real devices, with automated, visual, and AI-driven test options.

features.title: Real browser and device cloud
features.description: Access to 6,100+ real browsers and physical iOS/Android devices.

features.title: Live/manual testing
features.description: Interactive manual testing sessions on real devices and browsers.

features.title: Automated testing
features.description: Parallel automated test execution with configurable monthly minutes.

features.title: Visual regression testing
features.description: Screenshot comparison to catch unintended UI changes.

features.title: AI-driven and MCP-based testing
features.description: Codeless test creation via natural language, with REST API access through Model Context Protocol servers for tools like Claude and Cursor.

features.title: Accessibility testing
features.description: WCAG 2.1 compliance checks.

features.title: Broad framework support
features.description: Supports Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, Puppeteer, Appium, Espresso, XCUITest, and Maestro.

pros: Founded in 2011 with over a decade of operating history
180,000+ active users and 1+ billion tests executed
EU-hosted infrastructure with GDPR compliance
14-day free plan with no credit card required
Supports a wide range of automation frameworks and languages
cons: Pricing is in EUR, which may add currency conversion considerations for non-EU customers
Higher parallel test counts require the Automated Pro or Enterprise tiers
Enterprise plan requires contacting sales for pricing

faqs.question: Does TestingBot offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, TestingBot offers a 14-day free plan with access to all 6,100+ browsers and devices, no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is TestingBot based?
faqs.answer: TestingBot is headquartered in Moerbeke-Waas, Belgium, and hosts its infrastructure in the EU.

faqs.question: What automation frameworks does TestingBot support?
faqs.answer: TestingBot supports Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, Puppeteer, Appium, Espresso, XCUITest, and Maestro.

faqs.question: Does TestingBot support AI-driven testing?
faqs.answer: Yes, TestingBot offers codeless AI testing via natural language prompts and REST API access through MCP servers for AI assistants like Claude and Cursor.

faqs.question: Is pay-as-you-go pricing available?
faqs.answer: Yes, one-time minute packages are available starting at €60 for 1,000 minutes, and credits never expire.

use_cases.title: Cross-browser compatibility testing
use_cases.description: Test web applications across thousands of real browser and OS combinations.

use_cases.title: Mobile app testing
use_cases.description: Test iOS and Android apps on real physical devices.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted test creation
use_cases.description: Generate and run tests using natural language via MCP integration with AI coding assistants.

use_cases.title: Accessibility compliance checks
use_cases.description: Verify WCAG 2.1 compliance for web applications.

features: Unlimited live, visual, and automated testing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TestingBot?
paragraphs: TestingBot is a cloud-based testing platform, founded in 2011 and based in Moerbeke-Waas, Belgium, that lets teams run automated, manual, and visual tests across more than 6,100 real browsers and devices.

It supports major automation frameworks including Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, Puppeteer, and Appium, and offers codeless AI-driven test creation through Model Context Protocol integrations with AI assistants.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TestingBot is for
paragraphs: TestingBot suits QA teams and developers who need EU-hosted cross-browser and mobile device testing, from small teams starting with the free plan to larger teams running high-volume parallel automated tests.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted uptime monitoring tool with status pages and 90+ notification integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Uptime Kuma is a free, open-source self-hosted monitoring tool for HTTP(s), TCP, DNS, and Docker with 90+ notification integrations.

features.title: Multi-protocol monitoring
features.description: Monitors HTTP(s), TCP, WebSocket, ping, DNS, Docker containers, and gaming servers.

features.title: 20-second monitoring intervals
features.description: Frequent checks to detect outages quickly.

features.title: Status pages
features.description: Multiple public status pages with custom domain mapping.

features.title: 90+ notification integrations
features.description: Alerts via Telegram, Discord, Slack, email (SMTP), Pushover, Gotify, and more.

features.title: Certificate and ping tracking
features.description: Tracks TLS certificate expiry and displays ping charts.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs via Docker Compose, Docker CLI, or a non-Docker install on Linux or Windows 10+.

pros: Fully free and open source under the MIT license
Self-hosted, giving full control over data and infrastructure
Supports 90+ notification services
Actively developed with 89.2k+ GitHub stars and 130+ releases
Monitors a wide range of protocols including Docker containers and game servers
cons: Requires self-hosting and maintenance; no managed cloud version
Requires Node.js 20.4+ or Docker for installation
No official mobile app; deployment/monitoring config is web UI based
No AI-based features

faqs.question: Is Uptime Kuma free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Uptime Kuma is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Do I need to host Uptime Kuma myself?
faqs.answer: Yes, Uptime Kuma is a self-hosted tool, deployable via Docker Compose, Docker CLI, or a non-Docker install on Linux or Windows 10+.

faqs.question: What protocols can Uptime Kuma monitor?
faqs.answer: It can monitor HTTP(s), TCP, WebSocket, ping, DNS, Docker containers, and gaming servers.

faqs.question: How many notification services does it support?
faqs.answer: Uptime Kuma supports 90+ notification services including Telegram, Discord, Slack, email, Pushover, and Gotify.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted uptime monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor websites, servers, and services without relying on a third-party SaaS.

use_cases.title: Docker container monitoring
use_cases.description: Track the health of Docker containers alongside other infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Public status pages
use_cases.description: Communicate service status to users via a custom-domain status page.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Uptime Kuma?
paragraphs: Uptime Kuma is a free, open-source, self-hosted monitoring tool that checks the availability of websites, servers, and services over HTTP(s), TCP, WebSocket, ping, DNS, Docker, and gaming server protocols.
It is released under the MIT license and can be deployed via Docker Compose, Docker CLI, or a direct install on Linux or Windows 10+, with alerts routed through 90+ notification integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Uptime Kuma is for
paragraphs: Uptime Kuma is aimed at developers, sysadmins, and self-hosting enthusiasts who want a free, self-managed alternative to commercial uptime monitoring services.

enrichment.short_description: External monitoring for websites, APIs, SSL, domains, page speed, and servers, serving over 220,000 customers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: StatusCake monitors uptime, SSL, domains, page speed, and servers with a free plan and paid tiers from €16.66/month.

features.title: Uptime monitoring
features.description: Checks every 30 seconds from 30+ countries on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Page speed monitoring
features.description: Identifies performance bottlenecks and Core Web Vitals compliance.

features.title: SSL certificate monitoring
features.description: Alerts before certificates expire to prevent service disruption.

features.title: Domain monitoring
features.description: Tracks domain expiry and DNS changes.

features.title: Server monitoring
features.description: Tracks CPU, RAM, and disk usage with alerts.

features.title: Status pages
features.description: Customer-facing status pages for incident communication.

features.title: Google SSO
features.description: Available across all customer tiers.

pros: Free plan available with no time limit
Serves over 220,000 customers including Accenture, Audi, Mastercard, and Netflix
Covers uptime, SSL, domain, page speed, and server monitoring in one product
7-day free trial available on paid plans
Multi-year billing discounts of up to 40%
cons: Free plan is limited to 10 uptime monitors and 5-minute check intervals
30-second check intervals require the Business plan or above
Enterprise plan requires custom pricing and sales contact
Headquarters and company size not publicly disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does StatusCake have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, StatusCake offers a free plan with 10 uptime monitors, 5-minute test intervals, and one each of page speed, domain, and SSL monitors.

faqs.question: When was StatusCake founded?
faqs.answer: StatusCake was founded in 2012.

faqs.question: How many customers does StatusCake serve?
faqs.answer: StatusCake states it serves more than 220,000 customers.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day free trial is available on the Superior and Business plans.

faqs.question: What currencies does StatusCake accept?
faqs.answer: StatusCake accepts USD, GBP, EUR, AUD, NZD, SGD, and CAD.

use_cases.title: Website uptime monitoring
use_cases.description: Detect outages quickly with frequent checks from multiple global locations.

use_cases.title: SSL and domain expiry tracking
use_cases.description: Avoid outages caused by expired certificates or domains.

use_cases.title: Page speed and Core Web Vitals monitoring
use_cases.description: Track site performance for SEO and user experience.

use_cases.title: Server health monitoring
use_cases.description: Track CPU, RAM, and disk usage on servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is StatusCake?
paragraphs: StatusCake, founded in 2012, is an external monitoring service that tracks website uptime, API availability, SSL certificate validity, domain expiry, page speed, and server health.
It offers a free plan alongside paid Superior, Business, and Enterprise tiers, and is used by over 220,000 customers including Accenture, Audi, Mastercard, and Netflix.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who StatusCake is for
paragraphs: StatusCake fits small businesses starting on its free plan through to enterprises needing constant check rates, dedicated monitoring locations, and SLAs.

enrichment.short_description: Uptime, server, and blacklist monitoring platform with a free-forever tier for hosting providers and site owners.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HetrixTools offers free uptime, server, and IP blacklist monitoring, with 15 uptime and 32 blacklist monitors free forever.

features.title: Global uptime monitoring
features.description: Checks websites and servers from up to 12 locations worldwide.

features.title: Server metrics monitoring
features.description: Tracks server health via an agent for Linux, Windows, macOS, and shared hosting.

features.title: Blacklist monitoring
features.description: Tracks IP and domain reputation across major blacklists, including Microsoft SNDS.

features.title: SSL and domain expiration tracking
features.description: Alerts before SSL certificates or domains expire.

features.title: Nameserver change monitoring
features.description: Detects unexpected DNS/nameserver changes.

features.title: Public status pages
features.description: Customizable, white-labeled status pages.

features.title: Wide notification integrations
features.description: Alerts via email, SMS, phone calls, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, Zenduty, and webhooks.

pros: Free-forever tier with 15 uptime monitors and 32 blacklist monitors
Combines uptime, server, and blacklist/reputation monitoring in one platform
Server agent supports Linux, Windows, macOS, and shared hosting
Wide range of notification integrations including PagerDuty and Opsgenie
Operating since 2015
cons: Detailed paid plan pricing is not publicly documented on the marketing site
Headquarters and company size are not publicly disclosed
No AI-based features mentioned

faqs.question: Does HetrixTools have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, HetrixTools offers 15 uptime monitors and 32 blacklist monitors free forever.

faqs.question: When was HetrixTools founded?
faqs.answer: HetrixTools was founded in May 2015.

faqs.question: What operating systems does the server agent support?
faqs.answer: The server monitoring agent supports Linux, Windows, macOS, and shared hosting environments.

faqs.question: Can HetrixTools monitor IP blacklist status?
faqs.answer: Yes, it monitors network/IP reputation across major blacklists, including Microsoft SNDS.

use_cases.title: Hosting provider infrastructure monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor uptime and server health across client infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Email deliverability protection
use_cases.description: Track IP and domain blacklist status to protect email deliverability.

use_cases.title: Website and server uptime tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor websites and servers from multiple global locations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HetrixTools?
paragraphs: HetrixTools, founded in May 2015, is a monitoring platform that tracks website and server uptime, server health metrics, and IP/domain blacklist reputation from up to 12 global locations.
It offers a free-forever tier with 15 uptime monitors and 32 blacklist monitors, alongside paid tiers for higher monitoring volume.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HetrixTools is for
paragraphs: HetrixTools is geared toward hosting providers, datacenter operators, and system administrators who need combined uptime, server, and blacklist reputation monitoring.

enrichment.short_description: Website health monitoring covering uptime, SSL, DNS, broken links, cron jobs, and performance, with every feature on every plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Oh Dear monitors uptime, SSL, DNS, broken links, and cron jobs, with all features included on every plan from €13/month.

features.title: Uptime monitoring
features.description: Checks from multiple global locations as often as every minute.

features.title: SSL certificate monitoring
features.description: Alerts before SSL certificates expire.

features.title: Broken link and mixed content scanning
features.description: Detects broken links and mixed content issues on a site.

features.title: DNS and domain monitoring
features.description: Tracks DNS records and domain expiration.

features.title: Cron job monitoring
features.description: Detects failed or missed scheduled tasks.

features.title: Performance and Lighthouse monitoring
features.description: Tracks site performance using Lighthouse scoring.

features.title: Branded client status pages
features.description: Client-facing status pages with automated monthly reporting.

features.title: AI-powered checks
features.description: Includes AI-based checks among its monitoring capabilities.

pros: Every feature is included on every plan; pricing only scales with site/server capacity
Has completed over 67 billion checks and sent 38 million alerts
EU-hosted with GDPR-friendly terms and a DPA available
10-day free trial with no credit card required
30-day money-back guarantee
Built-in monthly client reporting suited to agencies
cons: No permanent free tier; requires a paid subscription after the trial
Pricing scales by number of sites/servers monitored, which can add up for large portfolios
Founding year and headquarters are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Oh Dear have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oh Dear offers a 10-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Are all features available on every plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oh Dear includes every feature on every plan; only the number of monitored sites/servers changes between tiers.

faqs.question: What does the Mini plan include?
faqs.answer: The Mini plan costs €13/month (annual billing) and covers up to 5 sites, with all monitoring features included.

faqs.question: Does Oh Dear monitor cron jobs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oh Dear monitors scheduled/cron jobs and alerts on failures or missed runs.

faqs.question: Is there a money-back guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oh Dear offers a 30-day money-back guarantee and lets customers cancel anytime.

use_cases.title: Agency client site monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor and report on multiple client websites from a single dashboard with branded status pages.

use_cases.title: Application health monitoring
use_cases.description: Track uptime, SSL, DNS, and cron jobs for production applications.

use_cases.title: Performance tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor site performance and Lighthouse scores over time.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Oh Dear?
paragraphs: Oh Dear is a website health monitoring service that checks uptime, SSL certificates, DNS records, broken links, cron jobs, and performance, and has run more than 67 billion checks to date.
Every plan includes the full feature set; pricing scales only with the number of sites or servers monitored, from €13/month for up to 5 sites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Oh Dear is for
paragraphs: Oh Dear is designed for agencies and developers managing multiple client or production websites who need centralized monitoring, branded status pages, and automated client reporting.

enrichment.short_description: Uptime monitoring platform with built-in status pages and on-call escalation, including a free forever plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hyperping combines global uptime monitoring, branded status pages, and on-call escalation, with a free plan and paid tiers from $29/mo.

features.title: Multi-region uptime monitoring
features.description: Monitors endpoints from global locations with configurable check intervals.

features.title: Branded status pages
features.description: Custom-domain status pages for customer communication.

features.title: On-call escalation
features.description: Escalation policies and alert routing for incident response.

features.title: Server agents
features.description: Agents for monitoring server-level metrics, with limits varying by plan.

features.title: Browser checks
features.description: Playwright-based synthetic browser checks on paid plans.

features.title: Alert integrations
features.description: Notifications via Slack, Microsoft Teams, PagerDuty, email, SMS, phone calls, and webhooks.

pros: Free plan available with 20 monitors and no time limit
14-day free trial on paid plans, no card required
Combines monitoring, status pages, and on-call in a single product
Annual billing includes 2 months free
Business plan supports SAML SSO and audit logs
cons: Free plan is limited to 5-minute check intervals and 1 basic status page
30-second and 20-second check intervals require paid plans
Additional seats on the Business plan cost $12/month each beyond the included 15
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Hyperping have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 20 monitors, 5-minute check intervals, 1 basic status page, and 1 server agent.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hyperping offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Essentials plan starts at $29/month and includes 50 monitors with 30-second checks.

faqs.question: Does Hyperping support on-call escalation?
faqs.answer: Yes, on-call and escalation policies are included starting with the Essentials plan.

faqs.question: Does Hyperping integrate with incident tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with Slack, Microsoft Teams, PagerDuty, email, SMS, and webhooks.

use_cases.title: Service uptime monitoring
use_cases.description: Detect outages across web services from multiple global locations.

use_cases.title: Customer-facing status communication
use_cases.description: Publish incidents and uptime history on a branded status page.

use_cases.title: On-call incident response
use_cases.description: Route alerts through escalation policies to on-call engineers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hyperping?
paragraphs: Hyperping is an uptime monitoring platform that combines global monitoring checks with built-in branded status pages and on-call escalation.
It offers a free plan with 20 monitors, and paid plans from $29/month that add faster check intervals, custom-domain status pages, server agents, and browser checks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hyperping is for
paragraphs: Hyperping targets engineering and DevOps teams that want monitoring, status pages, and on-call alerting in one tool rather than stitching together separate services.

enrichment.short_description: Monitoring platform for cron jobs, background tasks, uptime, and heartbeats, with a free tier and usage-based paid plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cronitor monitors cron jobs, uptime, and heartbeats with alerts on failure, offering a free Hacker plan and usage-based pricing.

features.title: Job monitoring
features.description: Captures status, metrics, and output from cron jobs with automatic failure alerts.

features.title: Uptime checks
features.description: Monitors websites and APIs from 12+ global locations with intervals as fast as 30 seconds.

features.title: Heartbeat monitoring
features.description: Collects pulse data from user activity, system events, and edge devices.

features.title: Status pages
features.description: Public or internal status pages to communicate reliability.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Real-user monitoring combining traffic, error, and performance data.

features.title: Alert integrations
features.description: 10+ alert channels including Slack, email, PagerDuty, and Opsgenie on paid plans.

pros: Free Hacker plan available with 5 monitors, no credit card required to start
Usage-based Business pricing scales with the number of monitors
14-day free trial on the Business plan
Combines cron job monitoring, uptime checks, and heartbeats in one product
Enterprise plan offers a dedicated engineer and priority support
cons: Free plan is limited to 5 monitors, 5-minute check frequency, and no SMS alerts
SMS alerts and premium integrations require the paid Business plan
Additional users and SAML SSO carry per-user add-on costs
Enterprise plan starts at $6,000/year and requires custom pricing
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Cronitor have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Hacker plan is free and includes 5 monitors with email and Slack alerts.

faqs.question: How is the Business plan priced?
faqs.answer: The Business plan is usage-based at $2/month per monitor, with additional users at $5/month each.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cronitor offers a 14-day free trial of the Business plan.

faqs.question: What does Cronitor monitor?
faqs.answer: Cronitor monitors cron jobs and background tasks, website/API uptime, and heartbeats from user activity or system events.

faqs.question: What does the Enterprise plan include?
faqs.answer: Enterprise pricing starts at $6,000/year and includes a dedicated engineer, priority support, and custom features.

use_cases.title: Cron job monitoring
use_cases.description: Get alerted automatically when scheduled jobs fail or don't run.

use_cases.title: API and website uptime monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor uptime and performance from multiple global locations.

use_cases.title: Heartbeat monitoring for background processes
use_cases.description: Track pulse signals from system events or edge devices to catch silent failures.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cronitor?
paragraphs: Cronitor is a monitoring platform that tracks the status and reliability of cron jobs, background tasks, websites, APIs, and infrastructure through job monitoring, uptime checks, and heartbeat tracking.
It offers a free Hacker plan with 5 monitors, a usage-based Business plan starting at $2 per monitor per month, and a custom Enterprise plan starting at $6,000/year.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cronitor is for
paragraphs: Cronitor is aimed at developers and operations teams who need visibility into backend systems, scheduled jobs, and web services to catch silent failures before they cause outages.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source observability platform unifying logs, metrics, traces, and LLM observability, with a free self-hosted edition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SigNoz is an open-source observability platform for logs, metrics, and traces, with a free self-hosted edition and cloud from $49/mo.

features.title: Distributed tracing
features.description: Tracks requests across services to identify performance bottlenecks.

features.title: Application performance monitoring
features.description: Monitors and helps troubleshoot application behavior.

features.title: Log management
features.description: Ingests and analyzes logs at scale.

features.title: Metrics and dashboards
features.description: Infrastructure monitoring with customizable dashboards.

features.title: LLM/AI observability
features.description: Tracks LLM calls, analyzes performance, and monitors usage.

features.title: Agent-native observability
features.description: Integrates with coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor via OpenTelemetry and MCP.

features.title: Alerts
features.description: Actionable notifications delivered to preferred channels.

pros: Free, self-hosted Community Edition with full access to core observability features
Open source with 25,000+ GitHub stars and 140+ contributors
OpenTelemetry-native, avoiding vendor lock-in on instrumentation
Usage-based cloud pricing with no per-user or per-host fees
Cloud plan is SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant with US, EU, and India regions
Discounted Startup Program for early-stage companies
cons: Self-hosting the Community Edition requires managing your own infrastructure and support is community-only (Slack)
Cloud pricing is usage-based, so costs can vary with data volume
Enterprise plan requires custom pricing starting at $4,000/month
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Is SigNoz free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted Community Edition is free and open source, with full access to core observability features.

faqs.question: How is SigNoz Cloud priced?
faqs.answer: SigNoz Teams (Cloud) starts at $49/month usage-based credit, with traces and logs billed at $0.30/GB and metrics at $0.10 per million samples.

faqs.question: Does SigNoz support LLM observability?
faqs.answer: Yes, SigNoz can track LLM calls, analyze performance, and monitor usage.

faqs.question: Is there a discount for startups?
faqs.answer: Yes, eligible startups under 3 years old, with fewer than 30 employees and under $6M raised, can get the Startup Program at $19/month.

faqs.question: Is SigNoz open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, SigNoz is open source with 25,000+ GitHub stars and 140+ contributors.

use_cases.title: Unified observability for microservices
use_cases.description: Consolidate logs, metrics, and traces in a single dashboard instead of multiple tools.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted observability
use_cases.description: Run a fully self-managed observability stack for cost or compliance reasons.

use_cases.title: LLM application monitoring
use_cases.description: Track LLM call performance and usage within an application.

features: Full access to core observability features
SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliant

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SigNoz?
paragraphs: SigNoz is an open-source observability platform that consolidates logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, alerts, and error tracking in a single interface, positioned as an alternative to proprietary tools like Datadog and New Relic.
It offers a free, self-hosted Community Edition and a usage-based Teams cloud plan starting at $49/month, plus custom Enterprise plans starting at $4,000/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SigNoz is for
paragraphs: SigNoz serves platform teams, developers, and DevOps engineers at organizations ranging from early-stage startups to public enterprises who need unified, OpenTelemetry-based observability.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source monitoring system with a dimensional data model and PromQL query language, a CNCF graduated project.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Prometheus is a free, open-source monitoring system with PromQL querying and alerting, and a graduated CNCF project.

features.title: Dimensional data model
features.description: Flexible time series identification using labeled metrics.

features.title: PromQL
features.description: A powerful query language for analyzing and transforming metrics data.

features.title: Rule-based alerting
features.description: Alerting handled through the separate Alertmanager component.

features.title: Independent server operation
features.description: Each Prometheus server operates independently with local on-disk storage.

features.title: Instrumentation libraries
features.description: Client libraries available across major programming languages.

features.title: Broad integration ecosystem
features.description: Hundreds of official and community-contributed integrations for extracting metrics from existing systems.

pros: Completely free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
A Cloud Native Computing Foundation graduated project, hosted by the Linux Foundation
Native, first-class integration with Kubernetes
Large ecosystem of hundreds of official and community integrations
Powerful, purpose-built query language (PromQL)
cons: No managed/hosted offering from the project itself; requires self-operation
No built-in long-term storage; relies on local storage or external remote-write integrations
No AI-based features
Founding year and organizational headquarters are not clearly stated on the site

faqs.question: Is Prometheus free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Prometheus is free and open source, licensed under Apache 2.0.

faqs.question: What license does Prometheus use?
faqs.answer: Prometheus is released under the Apache 2.0 license.

faqs.question: Is Prometheus a CNCF project?
faqs.answer: Yes, Prometheus is a graduated project of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation, part of the Linux Foundation.

faqs.question: What query language does Prometheus use?
faqs.answer: Prometheus uses PromQL for querying and transforming time series data.

faqs.question: Does Prometheus handle alerting itself?
faqs.answer: Alert rules are defined in Prometheus, but actual alert routing and management is handled by the separate Alertmanager component.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes and cloud-native monitoring
use_cases.description: Collect and query metrics from containerized and cloud-native infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Custom application metrics
use_cases.description: Instrument applications using client libraries and query the resulting metrics with PromQL.

use_cases.title: Infrastructure alerting
use_cases.description: Define alerting rules on collected metrics and route notifications via Alertmanager.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Prometheus?
paragraphs: Prometheus is a free, open-source monitoring and alerting system built around a dimensional data model, the PromQL query language, and independent servers with local storage.
It is a graduated project of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation, hosted by the Linux Foundation, and is released under the Apache 2.0 license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Prometheus is for
paragraphs: Prometheus is used by cloud-native organizations, Kubernetes users, and DevOps teams that need to instrument, collect, and query metrics for alerting and dashboarding.
